schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

address.streetAddress: G-131, Ground Floor, Phase 3, Spencer Plaza Mall, Anna Salai

schemas.description: TDS return filing by CA firm. Quarterly returns (24Q, 26Q, 27Q, 27EQ). TDS corrections. Form 16/16A generation. TRACES compliance. Virtual Auditor.

step.name: Step 4

acceptedAnswer.text: Q1 (Apr-Jun): July 31. Q2 (Jul-Sep): October 31. Q3 (Oct-Dec): January 31. Q4 (Jan-Mar): May 31. Late filing: fee of \u20b9200/day under Section 234E.

mainEntity.name: What happens if TDS is deducted but return not filed?

acceptedAnswer.text: The deductee (payee) cannot claim TDS credit in their ITR until the return is filed and processed by TRACES. Late filing also attracts penalty under Section 271H (\u20b910,000-\u20b91,00,000).
Yes. Correction statements for errors in PAN, amount, section code, or challan details. Also handle demand resolution arising from TDS mismatches on TRACES.

mainEntity.name: What TDS returns need to be filed quarterly?

acceptedAnswer.text: Form 24Q: salary TDS. Form 26Q: non-salary TDS (rent, professional fees, interest, contractor). Form 27Q: TDS on payments to non-residents. Form 27EQ: TCS (Tax Collected at Source). Due: July 31, Oct 31, Jan 31, May 31.

mainEntity.name: What is the penalty for late TDS return filing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Late filing fee: ₹200/day under Section 234E until the return is filed. Maximum: total TDS amount. Penalty under Section 271H: ₹10,000 to ₹1 lakh for delayed filing beyond 1 year.

mainEntity.name: What happens if TDS is not deducted?

acceptedAnswer.text: Disallowance of 30% of expenditure under Section 40(a)(ia) for business payments. For non-business: no deduction allowed. Interest under Section 201(1A): 1% per month from date of deductibility to date of deduction, 1.5% from deduction to deposit.

mainEntity.name: How to file TDS return online?

acceptedAnswer.text: Prepare return using TRACES RPU or third-party software (Genius, Saral, ClearTDS). Generate FVU file. Upload on TRACES portal with DSC. Verify with provisional receipt number. Correct errors through correction returns (C1-C9).

mainEntity.name: What is the TDS rate for professional fees?

acceptedAnswer.text: Section 194J: 10% for professional/technical fees (₹30,000 threshold). Section 194C: 1% for individual/HUF contractors, 2% for others (₹30,000 single payment / ₹1 lakh aggregate threshold). Section 194H: 5% for commission.
